column1: # Each left_val and right_val is an AQL expression evaluated to produce a value.
# out_val is the expected unified value (ignored when boolean_result is false).
# Different integers (same type, different literal values)
# None does not unify with any
# None does not unify with scalars
# None does not unify with parent types
# List element-wise narrowing (type Names in lists are type Literals)
# Map key mismatch - closed (fail when missing value cannot unify with None)
# Missing keys synthesise Absent — closed maps without `?` reject
# absence even when the present-side value is None. To allow absence
# the present side must be optional (`y?:None`) so the disjunct
# Optional field syntax ? — "None or absent, universally".
# Absent optional keys are OMITTED from the unified result (not
# forced to None). Explicit None at an optional key is still
# admitted via the disjunct(T, None) value wrap.
# Mixed nesting: maps in lists, lists in maps
# Cross-type Failures: list vs map vs scalar
# None does not unify with list or map
# Any unifies with list and map
# Typed list vs empty concrete list
# Typed list vs concrete list (fail - type Mismatch)
# Typed list with map child type
# Typed list vs bare list type Literal
# Deeper typed list child type Unification
# Typed map vs empty concrete map
# Typed map vs concrete map (fail - type Mismatch)
# Typed map with map child type
# Typed map vs bare map type Literal
# Deeper typed map child type Unification
# Mixed: typed list of typed maps
# Mixed: typed map of typed lists
# Single-element lists unwrap during map auto-eval, so use multi-element lists.
# Verifies the algebra of unification across every shape category,
# pinning: identity (T unify T == T), Any as identity element,
# Never as absorbing, None / Absent as degenerate, scalar-lattice
# narrowing, cross-shape incompatibility, list/map family rules,
# disjunct distribution, commutativity (each pair tested in both
# directions), and associativity (a u b) u c == a u (b u c).
# --- Identity: every kernel type unifies with itself ---
# --- Identity: concrete values unify with themselves ---
# --- Any as identity element: Any unify x == x (commutative) ---
# --- Never as absorbing: Never unify x == fail (except Never) ---
# --- None is degenerate: only unifies with None ---
# --- Absent is degenerate: only unifies with Absent ---
# --- Optional keys (the universal "? = None or absent" rule) ---
# --- Associativity: (a u b) u c == a u (b u c) ---
# Each pair of rows checks the same triple grouped both ways.
# If a narrows to b and b narrows to c, then a u c == c.
